directions: Directions for questions 1 to 5: Go through the data
question_text: Which country has shown the maximum percentage change in FEII in 1998 relative to 1997?

directions: Directions for questions 1 to 5: Go through the data
question_text: IF China’s FEI is 10 times that of India in the year 1998, which of the following is true for 1998?

options.label: a
options.text: China’s GDP is 70% more then India.

options.label: b
options.text: China’s GDP is 40% more than India.

options.label: c
options.text: China’s GDP is 50% more than India.

explanation: For the year 1998 FEIH For China, China = 0.048 ………..(1) GDP China FEIH For India, India = 0.0072 ………..(2) GDP India FEI China = 10 FEI India on dividing (1) by (2): GDP 0.048 20 China ×10 = = GDP 0.0072 3 India GDP 3 1 China = = 1.5 GDP 2 India So percentage difference between china’s GDP and India’s GDP = 50% (a) Profitability is non-increasing during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99. (b) Profitability is non–decreasing during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99. (c) Profitability remained constant during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99 (d) None of above. Keys

directions: Directions for questions 1 to 5: Go through the data
question_text: If GDP of India increased by 5%, GDP of China increased by 7% and that of Korea decreased by 2% from year 1997 to 1998, which of the following is/are true? I. FEI to china was higher in 1998 than in 1997. II. FEI to china was lower in 1998 than in 1997. III. India’s FEI has increases from 1997 to 1998. IV. FEI to Korea was lower in 1998 than in 1997. V. Korea’s FEI has increased in 1998 from 1997.
explanation: Since the comparisons are for the same country in each case, assume the GDP as 100 in 1997 in all cases. So, GDP of China, India and Korea in 1998 is 105,107 and 98 respectively. FEI FEII ×GDP 1998 = 1998 1998 FEI FEII ×GDP 1997 1997 1997 FEI 4.8×107 For China: 1998 = < 1 FEI 5.46×100 1997 FEI 0.72×105 For India: 1998 = < 1 FEI 1.71×100 1997 FEI 3.12×98 For Korea: 1998 = < 1 FEI 2.8×100 1997 So, II and V are correct.

directions: Directions for questions 1 to 5: Go through the data
question_text: Which of the following can be inferred from the given data?

options.label: a
options.text: China’s GDP is more than India.

options.label: b
options.text: China’s GDP is less than India

options.label: c
options.text: India’s GDP is less than Malaysia

explanation: It can be seen that all the values (FEII) are expressed as a ratio of FEI to GDP, the absolute values of GDP cannot be determined.

directions: Directions for questions 1 to 5: Go through the data
question_text: In how many countries has the FEI increased from 1997 to 1998?
explanation: Since only the ratios of GDP and FEI are known, the increase in one independent of other cannot be determined.

directions: Directions for questions 6 to 12: Go through the data
question_text: What was the average annual growth rate in production for all four sectors combined between 89 and 98?
explanation: You may note that apart from a few years for manufacturing and mining & quarrying, the annual growth rates in most years have been positive and near the 5 – 6% mark for all the sectors. The other options [a], [c], and [d] are too far away to be considered. Alternatively, for ease of calculation take the 1989 values for a manufacturing as 400, Mining & quarrying and Electricity as 300 each and Chemicals as 200. then, the final value for manufacturing would be nearly 580, that for mining & quarrying would be about 365; that for electricity would be around 550; and for Chemicals would be around 335. 1 Initial value of production = 400 + 300 + 300 + 200 = 1200 Final value of production = 580 + 365 + 550 + 335 = 1830 1 Overall % increase ≈ 52.5% 1 Average annual growth = 52.5/9 = 5.85%

directions: Directions for questions 6 to 12: Go through the data
question_text: When during the given period, was the maximum production level in the electricity sector achieved?
explanation: Electricity production has increased throughout (as growth rates are positive throughout) and the maximum production would be achieved in the last year, i.e., 98.

directions: Directions for questions 6 to 12: Go through the data
question_text: When, during the given period, was the minimum pro- duction level in the manufacturing sector achieved?
explanation: The production for manufacturing can be minimum either in 1989 or in 1992 ( when the growth rate is negative). If Manufacturing started with a production of 100 in 1989, then production in 1992 will be 100 × 1.04 × 1.01 × 0.97 >1 So, the production in 1989 was the least

directions: Directions for questions 6 to 12: Go through the data
question_text: For the given period, which sector showed the highest growth rate?
explanation: Electricity has shown the highest growth rate in all the years and therefore, would have the highest growth rate for the entire period.

directions: Directions for questions 6 to 12: Go through the data
question_text: In 92, the index for the chemical sector as compared to the index for the Electricity sector was
explanation: Electricity in 1992: 100 × 1.05 × 1.07 × 1.08 = 121.4 Chemicals in 1992: 100 × 1.07 × 1.03 × 1.06 = 116.8 1 In 1992, the index of chemicals is less than that of electricity by approximately 5.

directions: Directions for questions 6 to 12: Go through the data
question_text: By what percentage was the output of the manufacturing sector more than that of the chemical sector in 91?
explanation: Let Manufacturing in 1989 = 200 and Chemicals in 1989 = 100 1 Manufacturing in 1991: 200 × 1.04 × 1.01 = 210 and Chemicals in 1991: 100 × 1.07 × 1.03 = 110 210 – 110 Required percentage × 100 = 90% 110

directions: Directions for questions 6 to 12: Go through the data
question_text: When did the maximum percentage change in the production levels of the mining and quarrying sector take place?
explanation: As can be seen directly from the graph, the highest increase (that of 8%) for Mining and Quarrying was in the year 1998, relative to 1997.

directions: Directions for questions 13 to 18: Go through the data
question_text: If the total work force was 76 million whereas the total value added was 225 million, then which of the following had the maximum value addition per worker?
explanation: Values added per worker for various sectors will be proportional to following ratios: Central 10/6 State 3/6 Central/state 12/3 Public 8/8/

directions: Directions for questions 13 to 18: Go through the data
question_text: Which of the following sectors has the maximum fixed capital invested per factory?
explanation: Fixed capital per factory will be proportional to the following ratios: Central 5/8 State 4/4 Central/state 5/6 Public 6/12

directions: Directions for questions 13 to 18: Go through the data
question_text: If the variable is proportional to the number of employees and the production per employee, then for which of the following is the production highest?
explanation: If the variables are proportional to the number of employees and the production per employees, then we can say that the variable cost is directly proportional to the production. Since the variable cost is maximum for private sector, it will also have the maximum production.

directions: Directions for questions 13 to 18: Go through the data
question_text: 012% of its total investment as fixed capital, then how much did the government invest (in ₹million) in Maruti Udyog Ltd which forms 25% of the investment in the joint sector? (1US $ = ₹45)
explanation: One of the women spent 2517 – 1378 = 1139, she is Chellamma. This is the only possibility as we add ₹1378 even to the least amount ₹1193, we will not be able to satisfy all the conditions given simultane- ously. So, the table obtained is: Archana Chellamma Dhenuka Helen Shahnaz 2234 1139 1193 1340 2517

directions: Directions for questions 13 to 18: Go through the data
question_text: Maruti Udyog Ltd is a joint project of the Indian Government and Suzuki Motors Japan, each having equal stake. One fine day, the Indian government decides to disinvest from the venture due to losses occurring from labour problems. How many money will be disinvested?
explanation: From the above question the money disinvested will be ₹6450 million.

directions: Directions for questions 13 to 18: Go through the data

options.label: a
options.text: The number of government employees is more than that of the number of factories in the joint sector

options.label: b
options.text: The number of employees in the public is same as fixed capital of joint sector

explanation: Neither of the statements [A] or [B] can be inferred as we know only the percentages and not the actual values of the employees, factories or fixed capital.

directions: Directions for questions 22 to 25: Go through the data
question_text: The highest percentage of growth in sales, relative to the previous year occurred in
explanation: From graph it is clear that, growth in sales is negative for 1997–98. The change for 1996–97 is very small. We need to check for 1995–96 & 1998–99 For 1995–96 = (250 – 100)/100 × 100% = 150% For 1998–99 = (680-290)/290 × 100% = 134.5% So, the highest percentage change of growth of sales is for year 1995-96

directions: Directions for questions 22 to 25: Go through the data
question_text: The highest percentage growth in net profit, relative to the previous year, was achieved in
explanation: In 1995–96, the change is from 2.5 to 4.5, clearly it is more than 50% change in net profit. In none other option, this change is even 50%. So, the highest percentage change of growth of net profit is for year 1995–96 We must try to solve such type of questions without actual calculation to save time

directions: Directions for questions 22 to 25: Go through the data
question_text: Defining the profitability as the ratio of net profit to sales, IVP Ltd, recorded the highest profitability in
explanation: Profitability for 1994–95 = 2.5/100 = 0.025 Profitability for 1996–97 = 6/300 = 0.02 Profitability for 1997–98 = 8.5/290 = 0.029 Profitability for 1998–99 = 12/680 = 0.018 So, highest profitability was recorded for 1997-98.

directions: Directions for questions 22 to 25: Go through the data
question_text: With profitability as defined in question 24, it can be concluded that:

options.label: a
options.text: Profitability is non-increasing during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99.

options.label: b
options.text: Profitability is non–decreasing during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99.

options.label: c
options.text: Profitability remained constant during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99

explanation: Profitability for 1994–95 = 2.5/100 = 0.025 Profitability for 1995–96 = 4.5/250 = 0.018 Profitability for 1996–97 = 6/300 = 0.02 Profitability for 1997–98 = 8.5/290 = 0.029 Profitability for 1998–99 = 12/680 = 0.018 So, we cannot conclude the statements of options (a), (b) & (c)
